build_from.reason: Greenfield project - no Go implementation exists yet. All source files are pending creation in tasks 1-13 of the plan.
build_from.suggested_approach: Build from scratch following the epic plan architecture. The plan references two out-of-repo codebases (~/Documents/GitHub/llm-tools and ~/Documents/GitHub/claude-prompts) as conceptual reference only - they are NOT to be imported or vendored.

existing_patterns.pattern: Go coding standards: package naming (lowercase, single-word), receiver names (1-3 letters), error wrapping with fmt.Errorf %w, context.Context as first param, table-driven tests, *_test.go co-location

Architecture: black-box interfaces, single responsibility modules, primitive-first design. Anti-patterns: leaky abstractions, god objects, hidden side effects. Go-specific: panic safety in goroutines, defer cleanup, robust JSON-RPC error handling for MCP.

Use cobra.Command with RunE (returns error), ExecuteContext for global timeout propagation, MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive for --base/--merge-commit, exit-code logic centralized in main(). Root command name: 'atcr'.
Generic mcp.AddTool with typed args/result for schema inference; one engine, thin handlers; InMemoryTransport for tests; honor ctx in handlers; stderr-only output in serve mode (stdio owns stdout).
Always decode with NewDecoder().KnownFields(true) for strict config parsing. Use yaml.Marshal/Unmarshal for whole-doc round-trip; goccy/go-yaml is NOT needed.

net/http+encoding/json for OpenAI-compatible client (POST ${base_url}/chat/completions); os/exec for git (CommandContext, no shell -c); text/template for persona prompts (never html/template); context+sync for fan-out (sync.WaitGroup must drain on cancel); testing+httptest for provider mocks (httptest.NewServer with atomic.Int32 for concurrency verification).
GitHub Flow / Trunk-Based: feature/* branches off main, Conventional Commits (feat/fix/docs/style/refactor/test/chore), squash-merge, <400 lines per PR, CI gates merge.
Pinned versions: cobra v1.10.2, yaml.v3 v3.0.1, modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk v1.6.1. Tier: cobra=critical, yaml.v3=important, go-sdk=critical.

related_files.purpose: Project overview — currently documents the OLD structure (cmd/atcr-mcp/, pkg/, skill/ with skill.yaml). Plan calls for a complete rewrite around panel+reconcile architecture.
related_files.status_note: current README references 'Go 1.21' and binary name 'atcr-mcp' — both outdated by the v1 plan

related_files.purpose: Currently empty placeholder. Will be populated as v1 ships.
Existing .gitignore should be checked for: /bin/, /atcr, .atcr/, coverage.out, .DS_Store entries
CI pipeline currently uses Go 1.21, builds cmd/atcr-mcp/. Plan requires Go 1.24+ and binary path cmd/atcr/.
Authoritative Go coding standards — must be followed across all created files
Architecture principles (black-box, single responsibility, primitive-first) — drive package design
Pinned dependency versions (cobra v1.10.2, yaml.v3 v3.0.1, mcp-go-sdk v1.6.1)
cobra usage patterns, integration notes for atcr
MCP SDK usage patterns, InMemoryTransport for tests, stderr discipline
YAML parsing patterns, KnownFields(true) strict mode
stdlib package assignments (net/http, os/exec, text/template, context, testing)

integration_points.name: OpenAI-compatible LLM APIs
integration_points.description: All provider interaction via POST ${base_url}/chat/completions. Auth via Authorization: Bearer ${api_key_env} at invoke time. No provider-specific SDKs.

integration_points.name: Git
integration_points.description: Range resolution, diff, function-context, default-branch detection, shallow-clone guard. All via os/exec (no go-git). CommandContext for cancellation safety.

integration_points.name: MCP stdio transport
integration_points.description: StdioTransport is the only v1 transport for atcr serve. stdout is owned by the protocol; all human/log output in serve mode goes to stderr.

integration_points.name: Filesystem (review directory)
integration_points.description: All output goes to .atcr/reviews/<id>/ with manifest.json, payload/, sources/{pool,host}/, reconciled/. sources/ is an open extension point: any child containing findings.txt is a reconcile source.
integration_points.anchor: atcr review creates .atcr/reviews/<id>/ and writes id to .atcr/latest; atcr reconcile/report default to .atcr/latest

integration_points.name: Filesystem (config)
integration_points.description: Two-tier: ~/.config/atcr/registry.yaml (user-level: providers + agents) and .atcr/config.yaml (project-level: roster, payload mode, timeouts, fail-on). Precedence: CLI flag > project config > registry > embedded default.

integration_points.name: Agent Skill (orchestrator)
integration_points.description: skill/SKILL.md runs host-model review (writes sources/host/findings.txt in v1 format) and orchestrates atcr range -> atcr review -> atcr reconcile. Optional: adjudicate ambiguous.json clusters.
integration_points.constraint: no sprint knowledge; input is git range/branch/PR; output is the review directory

integration_points.name: CI gate (exit codes)
integration_points.description: atcr reconcile --fail-on <severity> returns nonzero when any finding at/above threshold survives reconciliation. Enables PR gate with no glue code.

architecture_notes: Greenfield Go project — no existing Go source. Plan calls for fresh rewrite informed by (but not extracting from) llm-tools and claude-prompts which live in different repos.
Target architecture: single Go binary (CLI + MCP server over one engine) + Agent Skill. No logic forks between CLI and MCP handlers.
Go module import path (from coding-standards): github.com/samestrin/atcr/. Internal packages at internal/{gitrange,payload,registry,llmclient,fanout,stream,reconcile,report,mcp}.

Dependency tree MUST stay small per epic constraint: cobra, yaml.v3, mcp-go-sdk, testify (recommended). Everything else is stdlib (net/http, encoding/json, os/exec, text/template, context, sync, testing, httptest, log/slog).
Go version 1.24+. Pinned versions per .planning/specifications/packages/registry.yaml: cobra v1.10.2, yaml.v3 v3.0.1, mcp-go-sdk v1.6.1.

NAMING DISCREPANCY: existing .planning/.config/config.yaml and .github/workflows/ci.yml reference 'cmd/atcr-mcp/' and binary 'atcr-mcp'. The v1 plan calls for 'cmd/atcr/' and binary 'atcr'. Sprint task 1 must reconcile: rename to cmd/atcr/ or keep cmd/atcr-mcp/. RECOMMENDATION: align to 'atcr' per the plan; the older 'atcr-mcp' references are pre-plan residue.
CI workflow uses Go 1.21 — must be updated to 1.24+. golangci-lint-action pinned to v1.59, golangci-lint-action@v6 — verify compatibility with Go 1.24.

Findings format is the public contract: pipe-delimited, versioned (atcr-findings/v1), 8 cols per-source, 9 cols reconciled. Strict severity-prefix regex for extraction. This format will be documented in docs/findings-format.md (task 13).

Six embedded personas installed editable via `atcr init`: bruce (generalist/correctness), greta (algorithmic correctness), kai (architecture/design fit), mira (production feasibility), dax (test coverage/error paths), otto (style/readability/idiom). Persona prompts are text/templates with payload vars.

Three payload modes: blocks (default, --function-context expansion for small models), diff (unified diff, most compact for large ranges), files (full head content with changed regions marked). Per-agent override in registry.yaml; one run can mix; manifest.json records who saw what.

Reconciler is the net-new core: deterministic Go pipeline (discover -> normalize -> cluster by FILE+LINE±3 -> dedupe via token-set similarity -> merge with REVIEWERS joined, SEVERITY max, PROBLEM/FIX longest, CATEGORY modal -> confidence HIGH=2+ reviewers, MEDIUM=single -> emit). ambiguous.json sidecar for gray-zone clusters (conservative unmerged default).

Range resolution decision tree: explicit --base/--head -> --merge-commit SHA (base=SHA^, head=SHA) -> auto (merge-base against origin/HEAD -> origin/main -> origin/master -> local main -> local master). Empty range is HARD ERROR before any provider call. Shallow clone produces guidance to run git fetch --unshallow.

Fan-out has parallel lane (default) and serial lane (rate_limited: true agents), fallback chain validated at load time (dangling + cycle = error). Partial-success semantics: error only if ALL agents fail. status.json per agent (ok | failed | timeout).

Test framework: testify (assert/require/mock/suite) per .planning/specifications/coding-standards.md. httptest.NewServer for provider mocks with atomic.Int32 high-water mark for concurrency verification. Table-driven tests with t.Run subtests. t.TempDir for review-dir fixtures. Fixture git repos built with os/exec in test helpers.
Test coverage target: ≥70% per plan success criteria. Combined coverage enabled in .planning/.config/config.yaml.
All cross-stage handoffs are machine-parseable files on disk (manifest.json, findings.txt, findings.json, summary.json, ambiguous.json). No implicit in-memory coupling between stages.

Source discovery is open: any child of sources/ containing findings.txt is a reconcile source. CI tools and the Skill can drop sources/<name>/findings.txt and reconcile picks it up with zero config. reconciled/ is output, never input.
No filesystem writes outside the review directory and config locations (per epic technical constraints).

files_to_modify.reason: Full rewrite: replace pre-plan content (cmd/atcr-mcp/, pkg/, skill.yaml) with v1 architecture (panel + reconcile). Document 6 CLI commands, MCP tools, Skill, findings format v1, payload modes. Include payload-mode guidance: blocks=default for small models, diff=most compact for large ranges, files=audit-style. Note Go 1.24+.
Update go-version from 1.21 to 1.24, update build command from cmd/atcr-mcp to cmd/atcr (or document the chosen name). Verify golangci-lint-action v1.59 compatibility with Go 1.24.

AUDIT COMPLETE (2026-06-10): Already covers bin/, *.exe, *.so, *.dylib, *.out, *.cover, *.test, coverage.html, coverage.txt, .DS_Store, IDE files, .planning/.temp/, .planning/.memory/. Gaps: (1) does NOT cover '.atcr/' — review directories with potentially large payloads could be committed; (2) does NOT list 'atcr' as a build artifact (only 'atcr-mcp' and 'atcr-mcp-ssee' which are pre-plan names). Add '.atcr/' and either rename to 'atcr' or add 'atcr' alongside the legacy names.
Currently empty. Add v1.0.0 entry on first release. Use Keep-a-Changelog format.

files_to_create.purpose: Go module: module github.com/samestrin/atcr, go 1.24. Pinned: cobra v1.10.2, yaml.v3 v3.0.1, mcp-go-sdk v1.6.1, testify (latest stable).
Go module checksums (generated by go mod tidy).

CLI entry point. Root command 'atcr' with subcommands: review, reconcile, report, range, init, serve. Centralized exit-code logic in main() for --fail-on CI gate. ExecuteContext for global timeout propagation.
Cobra command: atcr review [--id] [--base X --head Y | --merge-commit SHA]. Calls fanout engine. Manages .atcr/latest pointer.
Cobra command: atcr reconcile [id-or-path]. Calls reconciler. Supports --fail-on <severity>.
Cobra command: atcr report [id-or-path] --format md|json|checklist.
Cobra command: atcr range [--base X --head Y | --merge-commit SHA]. Pre-flight: prints resolution JSON.
Cobra command: atcr init. Writes .atcr/config.yaml and editable persona files from embedded defaults.
Cobra command: atcr serve. Wraps internal/mcp server in StdioTransport. stderr-only human output.

Decision tree: explicit --base/--head -> --merge-commit SHA (base=SHA^) -> auto (merge-base against origin/HEAD -> origin/main -> origin/master -> local main -> local master). Default-branch detection, empty-range HARD ERROR, shallow-clone guard with guidance text. Exposes Resolve() and Resolution JSON.
Table-driven tests for all decision-tree branches. t.TempDir fixture git repos built with os/exec.

Three builders: diff (unified diff), blocks (git diff --function-context expansion), files (full head content with changed regions marked). Byte budgets with deterministic truncation (drop whole files by size rank). Records truncation in agent's status.json — never silent. Payload template vars.
Edge cases: no-brace languages, generated files, renames, binary files, function-context fallback to plain -U<n> context diff.

Two-tier loader: ~/.config/atcr/registry.yaml (providers + agents) and .atcr/config.yaml (roster, payload mode, timeouts, fail-on). Precedence chain. yaml.v3 Decoder with KnownFields(true) strict mode. Fallback-chain validation (dangling + cycle = error). Embedded provider defaults.
Strict-mode catches typos, precedence chain, fallback chain cycle/dangling detection.

OpenAI-compatible chat client. net/http POST ${base_url}/chat/completions with Authorization Bearer header. Retry on 429/500/502/503/504 with ~500ms initial delay and 1.5x backoff. Per-agent temperature/timeout. API key resolved from env var at invoke time.
httptest.NewServer provider mocks. 429-then-200 retry, persistent 500, timeout via handler sleep past deadline.

Parallel lane (sync.WaitGroup, must drain on cancel) + serial lane (rate_limited: true, ctx check before each invocation). Fallback chain. Global-timeout context with per-agent derived timeouts. Partial-success semantics: error only if all agents fail. Per-agent status.json (ok|failed|timeout). Pool findings.txt merge. summary.json. Manifest + latest pointer management.
httptest.NewServer with atomic.Int32 high-water mark for parallelism verification. Fallback invocation, timeout, partial success, agent-name sanitization against path traversal.

Findings v1 spec parser/writer shared by fan-out and reconcile. Strict severity-prefix regex extraction (^(CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW)\|). Escape literal | to /. Pad short rows to full column count. Per-source 8 cols, reconciled 9 cols.
Table-driven tests: extraction, escape, pad, parse round-trip, prose-mention exclusion.

Deterministic reconciler pipeline: discover sources -> normalize (tolerate short rows) -> cluster by (FILE, LINE ± 3) -> dedupe via normalized PROBLEM token-set similarity with threshold -> merge (REVIEWERS joined, SEVERITY max with disagreement annotation, PROBLEM/FIX longest, CATEGORY modal, EST_MINUTES max) -> confidence (HIGH=2+ distinct reviewers, MEDIUM=single, LOW reserved for untrusted) -> emit findings.txt (9-col), findings.json, report.md, summary.json, ambiguous.json sidecar.
Clustering, dedupe similarity threshold, merge rules, confidence scoring, disagreement annotation, ambiguous sidecar generation, --fail-on exit-code mapping.

Three renderers over findings.json: md (executive summary + severity-grouped findings with problem/fix/evidence/reviewers/disagreements), json (raw), checklist (render-only markdown table, no persistence).
All three renderers over a shared fixture findings.json. Verify counts by severity × confidence and panel table.

MCP stdio server using modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk. Generic mcp.AddTool with typed args/result. Tools: atcr_review, atcr_reconcile, atcr_report, atcr_range, atcr_status. Thin handlers call the same internal packages as CLI commands (no logic in handlers). Stderr-only human output.
InMemoryTransport integration tests — call each tool, verify typed result, verify stderr isolation.

Agent Skill: SKILL.md with installation in .claude/skills/atcr/. Host review (writes sources/host/{review.md, findings.txt} in v1 format). Orchestration loop: atcr range -> atcr review (background, polled) -> host review -> atcr reconcile -> present report.md. Optional ambiguous.json adjudication and re-invoke merge. No sprint knowledge — input is git range/branch/PR, output is the review directory.
Embedded persona: generalist / correctness. text/template with payload vars.
Embedded persona: algorithmic correctness.
Embedded persona: architecture / design fit.
Embedded persona: production feasibility.
Embedded persona: test coverage / error paths.
Embedded persona: style / readability / idiom.
Base persona template (resolution-chain fallback after agent.md, before embedded).
Versioned spec for atcr-findings/v1. Columns, escaping rules, extraction regex, version-header policy (additive-only evolution).
Configuration reference: registry.yaml schema (providers, agents, fallback, rate_limited, payload, temperature, timeout_secs), .atcr/config.yaml schema, precedence rules, fallback-chain validation.
Diff vs blocks vs files guidance: when to use each, token cost trade-offs, persona scope rules per mode, per-agent overrides, byte budget behavior.
golangci-lint config aligned with coding-standards.md (staticcheck + errcheck enabled).
Example CI gate script: atcr reconcile --fail-on HIGH, block PR merge on nonzero exit.

integration_gaps.description: .planning/.config/config.yaml (build command), .github/workflows/ci.yml (build step when go.mod exists), AND .gitignore (lines 3-4, lists 'atcr-mcp' and 'atcr-mcp-ssee' as build artifacts) reference 'atcr-mcp'. The v1 plan calls for cmd/atcr/ and binary 'atcr'. Sprint task 1 must pick one. RECOMMENDATION: align to 'atcr' (plan is authoritative for v1) and update the planning config, CI, and .gitignore in the same task. Note: .gitignore already has 'bin/' which catches the binary if it's built into bin/.

integration_gaps.gap: Existing README.md describes the pre-plan structure
integration_gaps.description: Current README.md documents cmd/atcr-mcp/, pkg/, skill/ with skill.yaml and a focus on the MCP server only. The v1 architecture is CLI-first with the MCP server as a thin transport. README must be rewritten as a major task (not a minor update).

integration_gaps.gap: Existing CI is at Go 1.21, plan requires 1.24+
integration_gaps.description: .github/workflows/ci.yml uses go-version '1.21' and golangci-lint-action v1.59. Plan and coding standards require Go 1.24+. golangci-lint v1.59 may not be compatible with Go 1.24 — verify and bump if needed.

integration_gaps.gap: .gitignore does NOT cover .atcr/ (review directory) or 'atcr' build artifact
integration_gaps.description: AUDIT COMPLETE (2026-06-10): Existing .gitignore covers bin/ (catches build output if built into bin/), *.out, *.cover, *.test, coverage.html, coverage.txt. But it does NOT cover '.atcr/' (the .atcr/ directory contains reviews/<id>/{manifest.json,payload/, sources/, reconciled/} which can include large payloads and findings — should not be committed) and does NOT list 'atcr' as a build artifact name (only 'atcr-mcp' and 'atcr-mcp-ssee' from the pre-plan era). If binary is built into bin/, the bin/ rule covers it; if built to repo root as ./atcr, the rule is missing.
CHANGELOG.md exists but is empty (0 bytes). Add a v1.0.0 entry when the first release ships. Not a blocker for task 1, but should be tracked.
Coding standards require golangci-lint with staticcheck + errcheck. Need a repo-level .golangci.yml aligned with coding-standards.md.
The plan ships with six embedded personas installed editable via `atcr init`. These need to be created under personas/ and embedded via Go embed in internal/registry (or equivalent).
skill/SKILL.md and any helper files (e.g., installation script) need to be created. Plan specifies the Skill is installable into .claude/skills/atcr/.

integration_gaps.gap: Documentation files for v1 don't exist
integration_gaps.description: docs/findings-format.md, docs/registry.md, docs/payload-modes.md are task-13 deliverables. Findings format is the public contract; should be drafted alongside the stream package in task 8, not deferred to task 13.

integration_gaps.gap: No examples or CI gate demonstration
integration_gaps.description: Plan calls for 'CI gate examples' as a deliverable but no example files exist. examples/ci-gate.sh is a natural fit.

test_patterns.naming_convention: TestXxx for unit, TestMain for setup, table-driven with t.Run for scenarios
test_patterns.mocking: httptest.NewServer for OpenAI-compatible provider mocks; atomic.Int32 high-water mark for concurrency verification; small handler delays (20-50ms) to make parallelism measurable without flaky timing
test_patterns.fixture_strategy: t.TempDir() for review-directory fixtures; fixture git repos built with os/exec in test helpers
test_patterns.build_tags: Integration tests separated via //go:build integration at top of file

test_subsystems.covers: Decision-tree branches: explicit base/head, merge-commit SHA, auto-detection, empty-range hard error, shallow-clone guard
diff/blocks/files builders, byte budgets, deterministic truncation recording, function-context fallback, edge cases (no-brace languages, generated files, renames, binary files)
Strict-mode typo detection, precedence chain, fallback chain cycle/dangling validation, embedded defaults
httptest mock provider, 429-then-200 retry, persistent 500, timeout via handler sleep past deadline, env-var key resolution

httptest mock provider, parallel lane concurrency (atomic.Int32), serial lane ordering, fallback invocation, global timeout, partial success, agent-name sanitization (path traversal), status.json per-agent
Extraction regex, escape, pad, parse round-trip, prose-mention exclusion, both 8-col and 9-col formats

Clustering (FILE, LINE±3), dedupe similarity threshold, merge rules (REVIEWERS join, SEVERITY max with disagreement, PROBLEM/FIX longest, CATEGORY modal, EST_MINUTES max), confidence (HIGH=2+, MEDIUM=single), ambiguous sidecar, --fail-on exit code
All three renderers (md, json, checklist) over shared fixture findings.json
InMemoryTransport integration tests for each tool, typed result, stderr isolation

risks.risk: Deterministic text-similarity dedupe under/over-merges vs. prompt-based judgment
risks.mitigation: Conservative threshold + ambiguous.json sidecar (conservative unmerged default). Skill adjudication path. Tune threshold with fixture corpora from real multi-agent runs.

risks.risk: blocks payload builder edge cases (no-brace languages, generated files, renames, binary files)
risks.mitigation: Fall back to plain -U<n> context diff per file when --function-context fails. Explicit tests per case in internal/payload/builder_test.go.

risks.risk: files mode produces out-of-change findings that pollute reconciliation
risks.mitigation: Per-payload scope rules in personas; reconcile annotates findings outside changed ranges.

risks.risk: Token cost surprises for blocks/files on large ranges
risks.mitigation: Byte budgets with recorded truncation; docs steer large ranges to diff mode; per-agent payload overrides in registry.yaml.
Version header (atcr-findings/v1) from day one; additive-only evolution policy in docs/findings-format.md.

risks.risk: golangci-lint v1.59 may not support Go 1.24
risks.mitigation: Verify on first CI run; bump to compatible version if needed (latest stable as of June 2026).
